anchors.quote: help agents interact with the economy starting with e-commerce

anchors.label: #745 — Steve Kaliski, Stripe
anchors.quote: adapt to that new kind of buyer.

text: Once agents go parallel and autonomous, the human's verification capacity — not the agents' generation capacity — is the binding constraint

anchors.label: #761 — Zack Proser, WorkOS
anchors.quote: agents are not the bottleneck now and I think that's going to increasingly be the case, but we are.

anchors.label: #761 — Zack Proser, WorkOS
anchors.quote: our attention is still, you know, in meatspace, if you will, and it still degrades under load. It's still the hard constraint, essentially.

anchors.label: #758 — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
anchors.quote: we just want you to look at PRs that are ready for you in GitHub.

anchors.label: #758 — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
anchors.quote: instead of you reviewing changes, if the change is pretty easy, let's just approve it with an agent and deploy it behind a feature flag.

text: The gap that kills agent PoCs is the evaluation gap — no defined, continuously-measured definition of success — not the choice of model

anchors.label: #767 — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
anchors.quote: Second is the evaluation gap.

anchors.label: #767 — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
anchors.quote: Evaluation is basically specification for your AI system.

text: Agents fabricate having verified — they report success they never achieved — so the harness must supply real verification, not trust the agent's account of it

anchors.label: #766 — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
anchors.quote: There's no error, no warning, just the wrong answer.

anchors.label: #766 — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
anchors.quote: The agent gets blocked, it needs to please you and it makes things up.

text: Route each task to the cheapest model that can do it — tiered model selection by difficulty is accepted practice, not a frontier idea

anchors.label: #791 — Erik Hanchett, AWS
anchors.quote: don't use the most expensive model for everything you're doing. You want to use multiple different models based on the use case. And then try to route to it inside your agent.

anchors.label: #681 — Laurie Voss, Arize
anchors.quote: So you can do tiered model selection. You can do cheap models for simple queries and expensive models in your agent uh for complex queries.

anchors.label: #613 — Harrison Chase, LangChain
anchors.quote: route between uh language models so one model might be better than another you might want to use Claud because of its long context window or you might want to use GPD 4 because it's really good at reasoning

anchors.label: #692 — Guillaume Vernade, Google DeepMind
anchors.quote: flex and that's basically I don't care if that takes a long time but I want to to pay less. So you're going to have a 50% discount but your request can be uh can be delayed

text: Trustworthy judgment can be manufactured from cheap stochastic generation — sample-and-vote, multi-model consensus, and debate panels beat a single expensive call

anchors.label: #251 — Aakanksha Chowdhery, Reflection.ai
anchors.quote: the models to generate multiple responses and then do majority voting.

anchors.label: #116 — Leonard Tang, Haize Labs
anchors.quote: having LMS debate each other, having the weaker LLMs debate each other about what the stronger model is saying and seeing if that makes sense.

anchors.label: #093 — Alberto Romero, Jointly
anchors.quote: Second tier is a multimodel consensus. So we leverage a diverse range of models such as GBT4, claude and

anchors.label: #451 — How to Improve Your Agents (lit review)
anchors.quote: we using multi-agent debate to get reliable State evaluation instead of using single uh

text: Parallel agents need per-agent runtime isolation — a sandbox/micro-VM/worktree each — because containers are not a sufficient boundary for agent-generated code

anchors.label: #704 — Lou Bichard, Ona
anchors.quote: And only with having sort of the full isolation of a VM will you be able to effectively do this properly.

anchors.label: #623 — Maggie Appleton, GitHub
anchors.quote: It is also backed by a micro VM. So a sandboxed computer in the cloud on its own Git branch.

anchors.label: #151 — Rene Brandel, Casco
anchors.quote: if you just use containers, by the way, that's not an isolation layer in case anybody's wondering. Yeah. Yeah. Don't use containers for isolation.

text: Input tokens dominate agent cost — fix what you feed the model before you optimize which model

anchors.label: #792 — Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
anchors.quote: 90% of your AI cost is input. Files, search results, context you send in. Only 10% is output. The code the AI writes back.

anchors.label: #792 — Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
anchors.quote: may be 30% of the cost, but other 70% is what you feed it. Fix the input, the model choice matters less than you think.
